prompt: A cinematic, photorealistic close-up of a giant glass hourglass. In the top bulb, a frozen, jagged miniature continent of Antarctica made of white ice is melting rapidly. Clear water drips through the neck into the bottom bulb, where a hyper-realistic miniature of London is half-submerged. Moody lighting, overcast sky reflected in the glass, no text, no labels, highly detailed.
dataset_name: NASA Climate Change and Global Warming: Ice Sheets
insight: Antarctica is losing approximately 150 billion tons of ice per year, a rate that has accelerated sixfold since the 1990s, directly contributing to global sea-level rise.
